project.currentMilestone:
Executive Judgment Optimization and Executive Product Validation

---
project.currentPhase:
Executive Judgment Optimization

Discovery has completed the first generation of its Executive Cognitive Operating System and established a stable, benchmark-validated cognitive architecture.

The Executive Work lifecycle has now been implemented and benchmark validated.

The architecture is no longer the primary bottleneck.

Discovery now possesses an adaptive executive reasoning system capable of:

- understanding organizational reality,
- synthesizing organizational knowledge,
- identifying organizational constraints,
- generating executive recommendations,
- optimizing recommendations under organizational constraints,
- simulating organizational interventions,
- communicating executive reasoning,
- recording Executive Work,
- reviewing executive outcomes,
- learning from completed Executive Work,
- improving the Operating Model,
- and continuously improving future executive judgment through longitudinal learning.

Current development is no longer focused on expanding cognitive architecture.

Current development focuses on improving executive judgment quality by refining:

- recommendation quality,
- executive communication,
- confidence calibration,
- executive narrative,
- orchestration,
- executive trust,
- and executive product experience.

Architecture should expand only when benchmark evidence demonstrates a genuine missing reasoning capability.

---
project.currentObjective:
The Executive Work lifecycle has now been validated.

The current objective is improving executive judgment quality.

Current priorities:

- Produce one clear recommendation.
- Correctly identify the primary organizational constraint.
- Improve recommendation confidence.
- Improve executive narrative quality.
- Improve executive explainability.
- Reduce executive reading.
- Strengthen longitudinal Executive Learning.
- Increase executive trust.

The current bottleneck is executive judgment quality.

Not reasoning architecture.

---

pipeline.rule: Every new capability must connect to the canonical cognitive and executive pipeline or explicitly justify why it is independent.

capabilities.name: Evidence Ingestion
capabilities.description: Transforms investigation context and source material into structured V3 evidence that enters Discovery's canonical cognitive pipeline.
capabilities.notes: The local extractEvidence function in app/api/analyze/route.ts belongs to a separate legacy or noncanonical analysis route and is not the canonical V3 evidence producer.

capabilities.name: Organizational Observation Inference
capabilities.description: Infers meaningful organizational observations from available evidence and semantic context.

capabilities.name: Organizational Mechanism Inference
capabilities.description: Infers causal or operational mechanisms that explain recurring organizational behavior.

capabilities.name: Organizational Belief Formation
capabilities.description: Forms organizational beliefs from mechanisms, patterns, concepts, and accumulated organizational evidence.

capabilities.name: Organizational Theory Formation
capabilities.description: Forms higher-order organizational theories by integrating reusable organizational concepts with supporting organizational beliefs into coherent explanations of organizational behavior.

capabilities.name: Organizational Condition Inference
capabilities.description: Infers persistent organizational operating conditions from beliefs, mechanisms, theories, memory, and organizational relationships.

capabilities.name: Executive Assessment
capabilities.description: Synthesizes organizational state, conditions, mechanisms, theories, beliefs, prediction reflection, and investigation opportunities into Discovery's canonical executive-level assessment.
capabilities.notes: Executive Assessment is the authoritative executive synthesis consumed by the canonical Executive Understanding Synthesis capability. It integrates Prediction Reflection but does not recreate prediction reasoning.

capabilities.name: Executive Understanding Synthesis
capabilities.description: Transforms Executive Assessment into Discovery's single canonical persistent Organizational Understanding and consolidates it longitudinally across investigations.
capabilities.notes: This is the sole canonical producer of persistent Organizational Understanding. Investigation-level understandings and legacy belief-derived understandings remain available as supporting working cognition but do not compete for canonical executive understanding. This is a terminal cognitive capability consumed by Executive Projection, Executive Workspace, and Atlas rather than by another registered cognitive capability.

capabilities.name: Organizational Runtime Persistence
capabilities.description: Persists organizational cognition across investigations so Discovery can maintain longitudinal understanding. Runtime also preserves Executive Work, Executive Review, Executive Learning, Operating Model Improvements, and persistent strategy beliefs.

capabilities.name: Organizational Belief Evolution
capabilities.description: Strengthens, weakens, creates, or revises organizational beliefs as new evidence accumulates.

capabilities.name: Organizational Learning Profile
capabilities.description: Measures understanding growth, memory growth, belief stability, theory stability, retention, reuse, and learning velocity.

capabilities.name: Organizational Concept Formation
capabilities.description: Forms reusable organizational concepts by abstracting recurring organizational mechanisms, beliefs, and phenomena across one or more investigations. Concepts become the semantic building blocks from which organizational theories are constructed.

capabilities.name: Theory Validation
capabilities.description: Evaluates the dominant theory, competing theories, supporting evidence, contradictory evidence, confidence calibration, and falsification criteria.

capabilities.name: Investigation Opportunity Generation
capabilities.description: Identifies the highest-value evidence gaps and recommends investigations that could improve organizational confidence.

capabilities.name: Organizational Prediction
capabilities.description: Infers explicit future-state organizational predictions from organizational conditions, organizational state, longitudinal learning, previous predictions, and modeled organizational relationships.
capabilities.notes: Version 1 supports continuation, deterioration, improvement, and propagation predictions. Predictions preserve confidence, likelihood, causal paths, assumptions, confidence limiters, and falsifying evidence.

capabilities.name: Prediction Reflection
capabilities.description: Transforms organizational predictions into explainable executive future reasoning by selecting a primary prediction and preserving supporting cognition, competing predictions, assumptions, confidence calibration, falsifying evidence, and executive recommendations.
capabilities.notes: Prediction Reflection is the canonical producer of executive future reasoning. Executive Assessment consumes this object and should not reconstruct prediction logic.

capabilities.name: Prediction Outcome Evaluation
capabilities.description: Evaluates previous organizational predictions against observed organizational outcomes, measures prediction accuracy, calibrates confidence, and produces learning signals that improve future prediction quality.
capabilities.notes: Evaluates whether previous predictions proved correct after new organizational evidence is observed. Produces prediction calibration and longitudinal learning signals without recreating prediction generation or prediction reflection.

capabilities.name: Architectural Planning
capabilities.description: Analyzes Discovery's current cognitive architecture and recommends the highest-value architectural work while respecting capability dependencies, implementation maturity, overlap analysis, benchmark readiness, and product sequencing.
capabilities.notes: Ranks architectural opportunities, explains expected value and sequencing, identifies prerequisites and blockers, and recommends the highest-value next development step. It replaces hard-coded recommendation branches with explicit system-level planning while preserving duplicate-work safeguards.

capabilities.name: Architecture Recommendation Projection
capabilities.description: Transforms canonical architecture recommendations into presentation-ready system planning intelligence for Sprint Startup, Architecture Handoff, and future architecture experiences without recreating architectural planning logic.
capabilities.notes: Projects ArchitectureRecommendation into presentation-ready planning content. It must not rank opportunities, calculate architecture health, or recreate Architectural Planning. Sprint Startup and Architecture Handoff consume this projected object.

capabilities.name: Organizational Simulation
capabilities.description: Projects Discovery's current organizational understanding into a plausible future organizational state by applying an intentional organizational intervention and evolving current conditions, beliefs, predictions, and calibrated prediction performance forward in simulated time.
capabilities.notes: Version 1 preserves the current organizational state while calibrating simulation confidence using historical prediction accuracy. Organizational interventions now provide the canonical input for future causal propagation and scenario comparison.

capabilities.name: Organizational Intervention Modeling
capabilities.description: Creates a canonical representation of an intentional organizational change that Discovery can apply to the current organizational state when forecasting, simulating, comparing, and later evaluating executive decisions.
capabilities.notes: Version 1 represents intentional executive actions as canonical cognitive objects. Later versions will infer affected conditions, mechanisms, beliefs, assumptions, evidence requirements, and causal propagation pathways.

capabilities.name: Executive Simulation Synthesis
capabilities.description: Synthesizes completed organizational simulations, cross-scenario comparisons, executive scenario rankings, the canonical Executive Optimization Objective, and the final Executive Decision Recommendation into one canonical ExecutiveSimulation object for Runtime, Executive Projection, Executive Workspace, Executive Decision, and longitudinal learning.
capabilities.notes: Canonical synthesis capability for assembling existing simulation, comparison, ranking, optimization, and recommendation cognition into a stable executive-facing simulation object. It performs no independent organizational simulation, scenario comparison, ranking, optimization, recommendation generation, confidence calibration, or constraint reasoning. The producer is deterministic when generatedAt is supplied.

capabilities.name: Executive Decision Orchestration
capabilities.description: Coordinates Discovery's complete non-mutating executive decision cycle by synthesizing the canonical Executive Optimization Objective, generating and evaluating intervention options, simulating candidate organizational futures from the same baseline, invoking comparison and ranking capabilities, and assembling the final executive recommendation.  The Executive Decision Cycle now consumes deterministic longitudinal confidence calibration derived from reviewed executive outcomes while preserving recommendation ranking independence.
capabilities.notes: Canonical non-mutating orchestrator for the complete executive decision workflow. It composes Optimization Objective Synthesis, intervention generation, causal evaluation, simulation, scenario comparison, ranking, and recommendation synthesis. It must not recreate the reasoning owned by those capabilities.

capabilities.name: Cross-Scenario Comparison
capabilities.description: Compares completed executive scenarios from the same organizational baseline, preserving each intervention's condition changes, prediction changes, confidence, assumptions, and recommendation while identifying shared and differentiating organizational outcomes.
capabilities.notes: Compares scenarios but does not score, rank, or recommend an intervention.

capabilities.name: Executive Decision Ranking
capabilities.description: Deterministically scores and orders executive scenarios using organizational benefit, organizational risk, confidence, and scenario-level recommendation while preserving the reasons that produced each rank.
capabilities.notes: Ranking remains separate from recommendation synthesis. Given fixed inputs and timestamps, the ordering must remain deterministic.

capabilities.name: Executive Recommendation Synthesis
capabilities.description: Synthesizes the final executive recommendation from the ranked scenario set, preserving the recommended intervention, recommendation status, confidence, expected benefits, trade-offs, risks, assumptions, next-best alternative, and evidence that could change Discovery's conclusion. Recommendation confidence is produced through canonical confidence calibration, including Organizational Uncertainty, constraint completeness, and longitudinal executive learning.
capabilities.notes: Recommendation synthesis remains separate from ranking and preserves what evidence or constraints could change the recommendation.

capabilities.name: Executive Decision Recording
capabilities.description: Records the executive's actual decision after Discovery completes recommendation synthesis, preserving the selected strategy, its relationship to Discovery's recommendation, executive rationale, accepted assumptions and risks, confidence at decision time, expected outcomes, success criteria, ownership, and review timing as durable organizational memory.
capabilities.notes: Canonical producer for converting a completed non-mutating Executive Decision Cycle and an explicit leadership response into a durable Executive Decision Record. It preserves generated Intervention Option identity separately from executable Organizational Intervention identity, records accepted, modified, alternative, deferred, or rejected dispositions, and does not alter organizational cognition. Persistence is performed through saveExecutiveDecisionRecord() and persistOrganizationRuntimeState() without incrementing investigation count.

capabilities.name: Executive Work Lifecycle
capabilities.description: Transforms a recorded Executive Decision into executable Executive Work, evaluates observed outcomes through Executive Review, synthesizes Executive Learning, and produces Operating Model Improvements while preserving complete executive lifecycle ancestry.
capabilities.notes: Canonical post-decision lifecycle capability. It preserves Decision Record ancestry through Executive Work, Executive Review, Executive Learning, and Operating Model Improvement.

capabilities.name: Longitudinal Executive Judgment
capabilities.description: Improves future executive confidence by consuming reviewed organizational experience preserved as typed PersistentBeliefs while maintaining deterministic recommendation ranking.
capabilities.notes: Applies bounded longitudinal confidence adjustment from reviewed Executive Learning. It does not change scenario ranking and counts each Executive Learning event once.

capabilities.name: Optimization Variable Selection
capabilities.description: Selects and weights the canonical organizational variables relevant to an Executive Decision by combining explicit executive intent, organizational condition priority, causal-network influence, and condition confidence.
capabilities.notes: Version 1 deterministically classifies variables as primary, secondary, or protected and assigns explainable weights using executive priority, organizational priority, and network influence. It performs no simulation, ranking, or recommendation synthesis.

capabilities.name: Executive Optimization Objective Synthesis
capabilities.description: Transforms an Executive Decision and current Organizational Conditions into Discovery's canonical machine-readable optimization problem, including optimization variables, variable weights, success targets, translated constraints, preferences, tradeoff strategy, confidence, and confidence limiters.
capabilities.notes: Canonical producer of the machine-readable optimization problem. Version 1 translates success metrics and constraints conservatively, exposes interpretation limits, remains deterministic when generatedAt is supplied, and does not mutate the Executive Decision or Organizational Conditions.

capabilities.name: Executive Communication Synthesis
capabilities.description: Synthesizes canonical organizational cognition into concise, actionable executive communication by producing an executive headline, summary, supporting signals, meaningful changes, forecast, recommendation, uncertainty, and progressive-disclosure evidence without exposing internal reasoning mechanics.
capabilities.notes: Canonical communication producer for transforming Executive Projection cognition into executive-ready language. It suppresses implementation details and engine terminology, preserves confidence, uncertainty, supporting signals, meaningful changes, forecast, recommendation, and evidence, remains deterministic when generatedAt is supplied, and does not mutate its input projection.

intelligence.interpretation: Structural connection is inferred from generated capability traces. A capability is considered connected when its Runtime-to-Executive-Projection pathway exists, even when its current runtime collection is empty. Direct UI presentation is tracked separately and does not determine whether the capability is architecturally hidden.

gaps.interpretation: An unpopulated domain is an architectural coverage gap, not automatic permission to create a capability. Validate product need and overlap before implementation.

potentialOverlaps.interpretation: Potential similarity requires architectural review. Similarity alone does not prove duplication.

duplicateProtection.permanentRule: Extend existing capabilities before creating new ones. A new capability requires a distinct responsibility, distinct produced object, distinct canonical producer, and justified position in the pipeline.

recommendations.highestROI: Expose intelligence already produced but not structurally visible in the Executive Workspace.
recommendations.reason: 7 projected capability appear potentially hidden from the UI.
requiredPreBuildChecks: Search canonical producers and implementation files.
Determine whether the intelligence is already produced but hidden.
Confirm the proposed responsibility is distinct from existing capabilities.
Extend an existing capability unless a distinct responsibility is proven.

Do not recreate Organizational Observation Inference; extend CAP-PER-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Mechanism Inference; extend CAP-UND-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Belief Formation; extend CAP-UND-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Theory Formation; extend CAP-UND-003 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Condition Inference; extend CAP-UND-004 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Assessment; extend CAP-UND-005 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Understanding Synthesis; extend CAP-UND-006 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Runtime Persistence; extend CAP-MEM-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Belief Evolution; extend CAP-LRN-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Learning Profile; extend CAP-LRN-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Concept Formation; extend CAP-ABS-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Theory Validation; extend CAP-SELF-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Investigation Opportunity Generation; extend CAP-SELF-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Prediction; extend CAP-PRD-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Prediction Reflection; extend CAP-PRD-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Prediction Outcome Evaluation; extend CAP-ADP-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Architectural Planning; extend CAP-SYS-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Architecture Recommendation Projection; extend CAP-SYS-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Simulation; extend CAP-SIM-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Organizational Intervention Modeling; extend CAP-SIM-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Simulation Synthesis; extend CAP-SIM-003 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Decision Orchestration; extend CAP-DEC-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Cross-Scenario Comparison; extend CAP-DEC-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Decision Ranking; extend CAP-DEC-003 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Recommendation Synthesis; extend CAP-DEC-004 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Decision Recording; extend CAP-DEC-005 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Work Lifecycle; extend CAP-DEC-006 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Longitudinal Executive Judgment; extend CAP-DEC-007 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Optimization Variable Selection; extend CAP-OPT-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Optimization Objective Synthesis; extend CAP-OPT-002 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
Do not recreate Executive Communication Synthesis; extend CAP-COM-001 unless a distinct responsibility is proven.
recommendations.decisionRule: Do not create a new capability when an existing capability already owns the responsibility, object, producer, or destination. Expose or extend existing intelligence first.

startupInstructions: Review current milestone, phase, and objective.
Review capability ownership before proposing implementation.
Review potentially hidden intelligence before building new cognition.
Review overlap candidates before registering a new capability.
